key_insight: A coupled CEL simulation plus ANN surrogate can accurately predict and optimize friction stir welding quality in HDPE, especially when bottom preheating is used to stabilize heat flow and axial force.
engineering_relevance: The results provide actionable process windows for producing stronger, defect-reduced thermoplastic welds with lower trial-and-error in manufacturing.
method: 3D Coupled Eulerian-Lagrangian (CEL) model for thermal, force, flow, and defect prediction
Two multilayer perceptron ANNs trained on experimental temperature and axial-force histories
Process mapping across rotational speed, welding speed, and bottom preheating conditions
evidence_signal: The ANN models achieved R2 values of 0.91 to 0.99, and the CEL model matched experiments well for void formation, thermal response, and mechanical response.
watch_item: The process map is based on the tested HDPE parameter space, so deployment on other geometries, thicknesses, or thermoplastics will likely require retraining and revalidation.
paper_title: Computational and machine learning modelling approaches for weld quality predictions in friction stir welding of high-density polyethylene
